name: Justice Democrats
subject: Top Democrat walks back “genocide” comment
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter, just when
we thought the Democratic establishment finally found the spine to call a
genocide a genocide... they backtracked.

First, Rep. Katherine Clark — Democrats’ second-in-command in House
leadership — admitted that Israel is committing genocide against
Palestinians in Gaza.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140920?t=1002&akid=36943%2E1203867%2EpTQSSE ]Tweet from Prem Thakker: Katherine Clark, the second-most powerful
Democrat in the House of Representatives behind Hakeem Jeffries, just
admitted Israel is committing “genocide” in Gaza.

Then, just three days later, Clark walked back her comments.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140920?t=1003&akid=36943%2E1203867%2EpTQSSE ]U.S. News: No. 2 House Democrat clarifies comment about Gaza
‘genocide.’ “I want to be clear that I am not accusing Israel of
genocide,” House Minority Whip Katherine Clark told JNS. Jonathan D.
Salant.

This is pathetic, Peter, but it’s
not surprising. We can guess that someone from AIPAC complained about
this. Clark herself has received almost $703,000 from the Israel lobby
throughout her political career.

If AIPAC can demand Democratic leadership retract their words and
whitewash a genocide — they have too much power in the Democratic Party.

Their organization is funded primarily by pro-Trump far-right
billionaires, and they’re the biggest source of Republican money flowing
into Democratic primaries – using GOP megadonor cash to defeat progressive
members of Congress like former Justice Democrats Cori Bush and Jamaal
Bowman, and now targeting leaders like Summer Lee and Ilhan Omar.

Far too many Democrats are either silent on this issue or openly defend
genocide because they are in the pockets of AIPAC. If we want a Democratic
Party that actually stands up for human rights and opposes genocide, we
need them to stop taking checks from this corrupt lobby group ASAP.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140920?t=1004&akid=36943%2E1203867%2EpTQSSE ]Bernie Sanders was right when he said, “NO Democrat should accept money
from AIPAC.” Add your name now to demand your representatives choose
American values over right-wing billionaire influence by rejecting AIPAC
money.

Add Your Name

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250819-36943-footer&t=1005&refcode2=36943_1203867_pTQSSE&akid=36943%2E1203867%2EpTQSSE

Katherine Clark — Democrats’ second-in-command in House leadership — admitted that Israel is committing genocide against Palestinians in Gaza. [ ]Tweet from Prem Thakker: Katherine Clark, the second-most powerful Democrat in the House of Representatives behind Hakeem Jeffries, just admitted Israel is committing “genocide” in Gaza. Then, just three days later, Clark walked back her comments. [ ]U.S. News: No. 2 House Democrat clarifies comment about Gaza ‘genocide.’ “I want to be clear that I am not accusing Israel of genocide,” House Minority Whip Katherine Clark told JNS. Jonathan D. Salant. This is pathetic, Peter, but it’s not surprising. We can guess that someone from AIPAC complained about this. Clark herself has received almost $703,000 from the Israel lobby throughout her political career. If AIPAC can demand Democratic leadership retract their words and whitewash a genocide — they have too much power in the Democratic Party. Their organization is funded primarily by pro-Trump far-right billionaires, and they’re the biggest source of Republican money flowing into Democratic primaries – using GOP megadonor cash to defeat progressive members of Congress like former Justice Democrats Cori Bush and Jamaal Bowman, and now targeting leaders like Summer Lee and Ilhan Omar. Far too many Democrats are either silent on this issue or openly defend genocide because they are in the pockets of AIPAC. If we want a Democratic Party that actually stands up for human rights and opposes genocide, we need them to stop taking checks from this corrupt lobby group ASAP. [ ]Bernie Sanders was right when he said, “NO Democrat should accept money from AIPAC.” Add your name now to demand your representatives choose American values over right-wing billionaire influence by rejecting AIPAC money. name: Tristan Rader
subject: Statehouse District 13 Update
body:
Gerrymandering, workers rights, power outages and more. | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/tristanrader/statehouse-district-13-update?e=869bcf0641)
---
| | |
---
| | |
# IN THE NEWS
---
|
---
# **Standing with Doctors & Rising Star Workers Organizing Unions**
**Rep. Rader stands with workers.** That includes the brave doctors at University Hospitals organizing with SEIU to form a union—because our healthcare heroes deserve a voice on the job. Rader joins REI workers and the Teamsters in their efforts to fight for a fair contract. Also, café workers at Rising Star Coffee in Lakewood are pushing back against union-busting tactics to secure fair treatment and better conditions. Whether it’s in a hospital, the assembly line, construction site, or a coffee shop, every worker deserves dignity, respect, and the right to organize. It’s time we all stand up for workers' rights—and build a future where everyone can thrive!

* * *

**Don't miss the Fun!
Re-Elect Tristan Rader Fundraiser 8/21**
Rep. Tristan Rader is officially kicking off his re-election campaign with a grassroots fundraiser on **Thursday, August 21st from 5:30 to 7:30 PM at One Star Bar in Lakewood**. All are welcome—no one will be turned away for lack of funds. If you can't attend but still want to support the campaign, contributions are appreciated and can be mailed to: **Friends of Tristan Rader**
14837 Detroit Ave. #141
Lakewood, OH 44107
| [RE-ELECT RADER - Fundraiser August 21st RSVP](https://tristanrader.us15.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe296f262404fb3f98dc9e04f&id=57a140c926&e=869bcf0641 "RE-ELECT RADER - Fundraiser August 21st RSVP")
---
|
* * *
## **Rep. Rader Introduces Universal Health Care Bill**
Rep. Tristan Rader introduced HB 289 to establish a single-payer health care system covering all Ohio residents. The bill would guarantee comprehensive coverage without premiums, copays, or deductibles. Rader called the legislation a critical step toward ensuring health care is treated as a human right. He also spoke at the SPAN Ohio conference in support of the Medicare for All movement.

[](https://tristanrader.us15.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe296f262404fb3f98dc9e04f&id=2519ab52af&e=869bcf0641)_Cathe Caraway, Rep. Tristan Rader, Former Rep. Mike Skindell, Co-moderator Juan Collado_
* * *

---
|
---
### **Rep. Rader Calls for FirstEnergy Accountability Under New Reliability Law**
Last week, Rep. Tristan Rader joined a bipartisan group of fellow members of the Ohio House Energy Committee in urging the Public Utilities Commission of Ohio to fully enforce the new reliability and accountability measures in House Bill 15. The continuous outages Cleveland and Lakewood face are unacceptable, and this new law will give regulators better tools to hold utilities like First Energy accountable. Rep. Rader worked in a bipartisan manner to help pass HB 15, which repealed the corrupt HB 6 coal bailout and replaced it with strong new accountability standards for Ohio’s utilities. Ohioans have faced far too many power outages because companies like FirstEnergy have failed to invest in and maintain their infrastructure. HB 15 gives PUCO the authority to demand upgrades and hold these companies accountable. Rep. Rader is committed to making sure the law is enforced so that communities get the dependable power they deserve.
* * *
| [RE-ELECT RADER - Fundraiser August 21st RSVP](https://tristanrader.us15.list-manage.com/track/click?u=fe296f262404fb3f98dc9e04f&id=824eed073a&e=869bcf0641 "RE-ELECT RADER - Fundraiser August 21st RSVP")
---
|
---
|
---
### **Rep. Rader Joins Texas Reps to Fight for Fair Maps**
Rep. Tristan Rader joined hundreds of state representatives from across the country—including lawmakers from Texas—to stand against gerrymandering and for fair maps nationwide. The issue is especially urgent in Ohio, where the state’s redistricting process begins this September. The Ohio Redistricting Commission will soon draw new legislative maps that will shape the state’s future for the next decade.

Rep. Rader is committed to ensuring that this process is transparent and accountable, and that Ohioans finally get the fair representation they deserve.

### **What’s Next for Ohio’s Congressional Maps?**
Ohio’s redistricting process is kicking off again—and it could reshape the political map for years to come. Here’s how it works: **➡️ Step 1: Legislative Map (Now–Sept. 30)**
The Ohio General Assembly has until **September 30** to pass a bipartisan map.
* Requires **60% support in both chambers** , including **half of all Democrats**.
* If they fail to pass one, the process moves on.

**➡️ Step 2: Commission Map (Oct. 1–Oct. 31)**
The Ohio Redistricting Commission gets a shot.
* Must hold hearings and approve a map by **October 31**.
* Needs **4 of 7 votes** , including **both Democratic members**.
* If that fails, it goes back to the legislature.

**➡️ Step 3: Second Legislative Attempt (Nov. 1–Nov. 30)**
Legislature gets another chance by **November 30**.
* Again needs **60% approval** , this time with **at least one-third of Democrats**.
* If no map passes, there's one final route.

**➡️ Step 4: Partisan Map with Rules**
Legislators can pass a **simple majority map** —but it must meet strict rules:
* Can’t unduly favor a party
* Must avoid unnecessary community splits
* Must aim for compactness
* Must include an explanation of how these rules were followed

* * *

Tristan shares in-depth writing on his environmental work—like banning fracking in our parks and under Lake Erie—dives into the state budget, and tackles the big questions about the future of our democracy.

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Trump wants you to pay around $240 to simply file your taxes
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren for Senate
body:
It's like there's an “open for business” sign above the White House.

| | | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/216?t=3&akid=2621%2E4032634%2EIkGvvL)
---
For billionaires and giant corporations, every day under the Trump administration must feel like Christmas. Trump’s latest gift to CEOs and big business? **He’s planning to end Direct File, a new IRS program that allows taxpayers to file their taxes with the government for free.** I’m proud to have helped create Direct File under the Biden administration’s Inflation Reduction Act — and even prouder of the results we’ve seen so far. Taxpayers had historically wasted time and money on filing their taxes through companies like Intuit (which owns TurboTax) and H&R Block — spending an average of $240 and 13 hours to file their taxes every year. It simply doesn’t have to be this way. To date, Direct File has helped over 140,000 Americans save $5.6 million in tax preparation fees. But instead of helping taxpayers keep that $240 back in their pocket, Trump wants companies like Intuit to continue raking it in and monopolizing tax preparation services. Why, oh why, would Donald Trump do that? Corruption. Intuit donated $1 million to Trump’s inauguration fund — and we can be sure that they are expecting a return on that investment. **Plain and simple: Trump is going after Direct File because the program stops giant tax prep companies from ripping off taxpayers for services that can easily be free.** [**Direct File made life easier and cheaper for Americans. Now Trump is killing it. I’m going to keep calling this out for what it is — greed and corruption, plain and simple. If you’re with me, add your name to say you want politicians to do everything they can to protect and expand Direct File, not dismantle it.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/signup/ew-em-directfile25/?source=EMZ27D_TAX_E_20250819_V1S1&t=8&akid=2621%2E4032634%2EIkGvvL) | [**ADD YOUR NAME**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/signup/ew-em-directfile25/?source=EMZ27D_TAX_E_20250819_V1S1&t=9&akid=2621%2E4032634%2EIkGvvL)
---
This giveaway to the big tax prep companies is basically putting an “open for business” sign above the White House. It’s a clear example of big money interests buying the president’s favor in order to get special treatment.
Shutting down Direct File isn’t good for the everyday consumer. It isn’t good for competition. It isn’t good for just about everybody except for the executives at a handful of companies — and Donald Trump himself.
It also harms the public trust. Voters are seeing how politicians can be easily bought and sold by the highest bidder. Whether it’s Paramount ending Colbert’s show just days after he criticized their settlement with Trump, or the Trump administration using trade negotiations to strike deals for Starlink — this kind of self-dealing erodes voters’ faith in their government. It means the rich and powerful win while everybody else loses out.
[**That’s why we have to get loud and call out this attack on Direct File to make it clear this won’t help the vast majority of Americans. If you agree, add your name here, and we’ll fight together to stop the Trump administration’s attacks on the Direct File program.**](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3124?t=10&akid=2621%2E4032634%2EIkGvvL)
Thanks for being a part of this,
Elizabeth

---
| | | |
---
| [DONATE](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/3124?t=14&akid=2621%2E4032634%2EIkGvvL)
---
| [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/365?t=4&akid=2621%2E4032634%2EIkGvvL) | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/367?t=5&akid=2621%2E4032634%2EIkGvvL) | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/368?t=6&akid=2621%2E4032634%2EIkGvvL) | [ ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/go/369?t=7&akid=2621%2E4032634%2EIkGvvL)

name: Ken Martin
subject: Democrats are done fighting with one arm tied behind our back
body:
---
Derek, Donald Trump is so terrified about Republicans losing power next year that he’s trying to rig the game at halftime with gerrymandered maps in places like Texas. For months, we promised that if Republicans wanted a showdown, they would get a showdown. Well, last week, Gavin Newsom and California Democrats sent a booming message to the entire country: **We are done fighting with one arm tied behind our back.** California Democrats are calling for a special election to pass the Election Rigging Response Act — which will redraw the state’s maps and defend fair representation in our country. This is a game changer. The Election Rigging Response Act will level the playing field and help ensure voters decide who runs the House of Representatives — _not Donald Trump._ The DNC will support this ballot measure every step of the way, but with Republicans undoubtedly about to throw everything they’ve got at stopping it, **I’m personally asking you to contribute to help Democrats win:** $3 to help defend our democracy. $3 to ensure voters pick their representatives, not the other way around. $3 to make sure Trump can’t rig the 2026 election to prevent us from electing a Democratic House of Representatives. [**Please use this link to pitch in $3 — or any amount that makes sense for you — today. We’re looking for a lot of contributions today to send a powerful message to Trump about the strength of our opposition.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?amount=3&refcode=em_20250819_4363_fr&refcode2=4363_22930333_EQSn6e&akid=4363%2E22930333%2EEQSn6e) _If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly._ | | | **[Donate $3](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em-dnc-august-2025?express_lane=true&amount=3&refcode=em_20250819_4363_fr&refcode2=4363_22930333_EQSn6e&akid=4363%2E22930333%2EEQSn6e)**
